purpose: Calibration corpus for the subjective design-review gate. Before the judge's 0-1 score on a real screen is allowed to GATE, it must score these anchors and separate good from slop. lib/calibration.mjs checkCalibration() requires every 'good' anchor to beat every 'slop' anchor by `margin`; otherwise the real-screen score is advisory and the deterministic lint floor stays the only gate.
render: Host-side. The package ships no browser. For each anchor the host renders `source` to a screenshot and passes it to the judge, which scores it with references/rubric.md. The judge must never see the `kind` or `expectedBand` of an anchor while scoring it.
howToFill: FILLED (bootstrap corpus, 2026-06-30). Good anchors are live product URLs the maintainer holds as the bar; slop anchors are self-contained HTML files in slop/ that deliberately violate the fleet's rules (do not improve them). To re-tune calibration to a specific project, replace the good URLs with that project's VISION.md canonical-example screens.

anchors.label: Linear — product/marketing site
anchors.why: exemplary — intentional hierarchy, custom type, considered spacing and a clear point of view

anchors.label: Raycast — product/marketing site
anchors.why: exemplary craft and restraint; dark-theme discipline, dense but calm

anchors.label: Curated Supply — editorial gallery/shop
anchors.why: confident, distinctive, editorial point of view; consistent primitives

anchors.label: Default admin template (stock cards + bordered table + Submit)
anchors.why: no point of view, stock components, identical card grid, pure #fff/#000, default look

anchors.label: Generic AI-default landing (Inter, purple-on-white gradient)
anchors.why: the exact generic-AI aesthetic the fleet exists to avoid

anchors.label: Near-unstyled form (placeholder-only labels, 'Submit', raw error)
anchors.why: system fonts, no hierarchy, raw spacing, blaming error copy
